sanskrit: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता । मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
hindiTranslation: दैवीसम्पत्ति मुक्तिके लिये और आसुरीसम्पत्ति बन्धनके लिये है ।  हे पाण्डव तुम दैवीसम्पत्तिको प्राप्त हुए हो, इसलिये तुम्हें शोक चिन्ता नहीं करना चाहिये ॥
englishTranslation: The divine nature leads to freedom; the demonic nature leads to bondage. Do not grieve, Pandava. You are born with the divine nature.
essence: Freedom grows from one kind of character; bondage grows from another.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Qualities that uplift a person lead to freedom, while qualities rooted in ego create bondage. Krishna tells Arjuna not to despair, because Arjuna already has the qualities that lead upward.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing your deeper orientation already points toward freedom.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, after listing the traits of the divine and the demonic, Krishna turns to Arjuna directly. The battlefield is still frozen, but the teaching sharpens: Arjuna is not trapped by fear. His deeper nature already belongs to the side that frees.
modernRelevance: You catch yourself spiralling after a harsh conversation, wondering whether you are becoming the kind of person you fear. This verse says to stop and remember what you are already leaning toward.
translationLiteral: दैवी (divine) / सम्पत् (nature or wealth) / विमोक्षाय (for freedom) / निबन्धाय (for bondage) / आसुरी (demonic) / मता (is held to be) / मा (do not) / शुचः (grieve) / सम्पदम् (nature or wealth) / दैवीम् (divine) / अभिजातः (born with) / असि (you are) / पाण्डव (Pandava)
insight: It shifts attention from self-doubt to the qualities you are cultivating, so you can align with what frees rather than what binds.

quoteVariants.text: The divine nature frees. The demonic nature binds.
Divine nature frees; demonic nature binds.
You were born for freedom, not bondage.
Choose the nature that frees you.
